topic.what: How architectural design choices (role config, topology, memory) affect security-performance tradeoffs in multi-agent LLM systems
topic.how: Stagewise evaluations across 13 architectural configurations using adapted BrowserART, OS-Harm, RedCode-Gen benchmarks
topic.why_matters: Multi-agent architectures introduce attack surfaces not present in single-agent settings; capability evaluation alone provides no signal about architectural security risks

characteristics.evidence: Table 1: Star+4 Specialists reaches 31% HT vs standalone 10% on BrowserART; RedCode-Gen chain reaches 42.5%

characteristics.vs_baseline: Specialist configs match or exceed standalone
characteristics.evidence: BrowserART specialist configs achieve 95-98% vs standalone 92.9%
BrowserART: standalone 75% → Star+4 Specialists 45%; RedCode-Gen chain collapses to 8.1%

characteristics.vs_baseline: Multi-agent shifts safety reasoning to execution
characteristics.evidence: RedCode-Gen: execution refusal rises to 38.8% as planning refusal drops

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success relative to benign accuracy
characteristics.evidence: Qwen3-VL: 95-100% benign accuracy while HT rises 9%→40%

characteristics.what_it_measures: Security degradation under architectural changes by model
characteristics.evidence: GPT-5.4: ≤3% HT across configs; Qwen3-VL: 9%→40% under specialization

good_for: architectural security evaluation for multi-agent systems

novelty: First controlled comparison of multi-agent architectural design choices for security
Adaptation of 3 single-agent benchmarks to multi-agent settings
Stage-wise metrics distinguishing planning refusal from execution-stage interception
Quantification of security-performance decoupling across 13 configurations and 6 models
open_problems: Universal architectural safety recommendations not possible due to scenario-dependence
Interaction effects between role/topo/memory not fully explored
Inter-agent communication protocols (A2A, MCP) effects not studied
Safety mechanism integration (monitor agents, guardrails) not evaluated
